records.summary: Four ultrasound images of the abdomen showing the right and left kidneys, gallbladder, liver, and ureters with measurements.
key_observations: Images show bilateral kidneys with annotations 'RT Kidney' and 'LT Kidney'
Gallbladder (GB) and liver are visible in one image
Ureters (UT) are shown with measurements: 7.07 cm, 3.48 cm, and 4.63 cm
Ultrasound machine settings (2D HAR, TOM 4.4, FRE 4.4, etc.) are visible on each image

records.body_region: Abdomen — hepatobiliary system, kidneys, urinary bladder, uterus, ovaries
records.stage_evidence: Clinical report of findings from ultrasound examination

ocr.verbatim:
Reg. No. :

Patient Name: 

Ref by: - Dr. 

FEMALE ULTRASONOGRAPHY REPORT

Liver: Normal in shape, size and normal echo pattern.
IHBR not dilated Portal vein not dilated. No mass / cyst seen.

Gall bladder: Normal in shape, size. Wall thickness appears normal.
Peri-cholecystic area appears normal. Multiple hard echodence shadow largest one (approx 8.7mm ) seen in lumen & neck.
Not dilated.

Spleen: Normal in shape size and echo pattern. No mass / cyst seen.
Splenic vessels not dilated.

Pancreas: Normal in shape and echo pattern. No mass / cyst seen.
Pancreatic ducts not dilated.

RT. Kidney : Normal in shape & size and location.
Corticomedullary differentiation is well distinct. Calyces not dilated.
No hydronephrosis or renal calculus seen in both the kidneys.

LT. Kidney : Normal in shape & size and location.
Corticomedullary differentiation is well distinct. Calyces not dilated.
No hydronephrosis or renal calculus seen in both the kidneys.

U. Bladder: Is partially filled.

Uterus: Normal in size (70x34x45mm) Echo texture of myometrium is homogenous.
Cavity empty. Endometrial thickness appears normal.

Ovaries: Normal.
Adenexas: Normal.

POD: No free fluid seen.

Lymph nodes: Not seen.

Ascetic Fluid: No free fluid seen inside the peritoneal cavity.

Others: Excessive gas filled bowel loops seen in all over abdomen.

INFERRENCE: Cholelithiasis.

USG has its own limitations, please correlate clinically
Not for medico-legal use

Mob.
E-mail :

records.summary: This is a female ultrasound report dated 19/03/2026, detailing findings from an abdominal ultrasound. The report notes normal findings for liver, spleen, pancreas, kidneys, and uterus, but identifies multiple gallstones (largest 8.7mm) in the gallbladder, leading to the inference of cholelithiasis.
key_observations: Multiple gallstones (largest 8.7mm) detected in the gallbladder
Normal liver, spleen, pancreas, kidneys, and uterus
Uterus is normal in size (70x34x45mm) with empty cavity
Report explicitly states 'Not for medico-legal use'
